_about: The closed vocabulary + component DAG (docs/CONTRACT.md is the law; this file is the data). One file, two consumers: Subsystem.Analyzers reads it as an AdditionalFile (SS011-SS016); the Registrar seeds it into the namespace at boot (\System\Components). Registering anything here is a deliberate event.

Vom.name: Virtual Object Manager
Vom.note: the kernel; the one node type lives here; logs through Dg

Dg.name: Diagnostics
Dg.note: records + ring; sinks are host drivers. Today reaches Rb/Rs/SessionManager - ratchet R3

Cm.name: Configuration Manager
Cm.note: volatile + durable planes; durable plane is a driver grant. Today probes MainActivity - ratchet R3

Rb.name: RuntimeBroker
Rb.note: brokers inference runtimes (LiteRT/ONNX/GGML) behind one Runtime contract; routes/authorizes/owns lifecycle, zero knobs

Rs.name: Remote Sessions
Rs.note: the PSRP subsystem (Rs.cs:14); single file pending Host/ assignment

Device.name: Device Drivers
Device.note: hardware/OS interop drivers, each mounted as a VOM leaf (Light, Morse, Surfaces, WebDrive, VomInterop). Registered for the namespace grammar (SS011); FOLDER OMITTED so SS014 DAG attribution stays DEFERRED — these drivers legitimately reach the host (MainActivity) today; their reference cleanup is a separate migration, not this addition.

Pwsh.name: PowerShell Surface
Pwsh.note: the in-process runspace surface: SessionManager, ReplEngine, Translator, HelpSystem, cmdlet families. Folder omitted — SS014 deferred (same reason as Device).

Cmdlets.name: Cmdlet Families
Cmdlets.note: PSCmdlet families under Pwsh (sub-namespace Subsystem.Pwsh.Cmdlets). Registered so the grammar resolves EVERY segment; folder omitted (SS014 deferred).

Zoo.name: Cmdlet Zoo
Zoo.note: asset-loaded auxiliary cmdlets (sub-namespace Subsystem.Pwsh.Cmdlets.Zoo). Folder omitted (SS014 deferred).

_synchronousCore_about: Components that are brutally synchronous — SS018 refuses async/await + sync-over-async here. The core is real threads + Fence handoff (push, best-effort, copy-then-share); async legitimately lives at the seams (Rb=LLM, Rs=pwsh, Host=web, Adb=network). This list ratchets OUTWARD deliberately as a seam is made synchronous — it never silently widens.

unassigned._about: Code that exists but whose component registration is a pending deliberate decision - do NOT coin codes for these in PRs.
areas: Host/ (ObpHost, ProjectionServer, Registrar, SubsystemApi, ModelsApi, Firewall) - ratchet R7

types._note: Descriptor/Routine/Dispatch/Information/Value/Directory/Type/Callback adopted 2026-06-12 from the primary-sourced NT taxonomy (SECURITY_DESCRIPTOR, IoCompletionRoutine, DispatchRoutine, FILE_BASIC_INFORMATION, CM_KEY_VALUE, OBJECT_DIRECTORY, OBJECT_TYPE, CALLBACK_OBJECT).

verbs._doc: SS013 requires every C# method name to START with a verb from one of the three buckets below (the FIRST PascalCase word only; the rest is the noun). A leading word in NONE of them fails the build. This governs C# method names ONLY — it does not touch the PowerShell the model runs at runtime, which uses pwsh's own Get-Verb set freely.
verbs._approvedDoc: PREFER these — the canonical NT-mechanism vocabulary (Alloc/Free/Spawn/Mount/Bind/Grant/Signal/Wait). The blessed, deliberate verbs the system is built on.
verbs._pwshDoc: PowerShell cmdlet verbs (Get-Verb-aligned), kept in their OWN bucket because they exist for a different reason: C# PSCmdlet classes must be named to match the shell (Invoke-Agent, Get-AdbProcess, Start-X). Allowed for cmdlet-facing names; non-cmdlet C# should still prefer the approved NT verb (Get->Query).
verbs._triageDoc: Verbs in the code but NOT blessed — a cleanup backlog to drain toward 'approved' or remove. They pass the gate today so the ratchet tracks every existing prefix, but each owes a rename: 'Describe' is retiring for 'contextualize'; 'List'->'Enumerate', 'Delete'->'Free'/'Remove', 'Run'/'Exec'/'Execute'->'Invoke'; predicates (Is/Should/Granted/Active) and nouns (Total/Cpu/Local) owe a Query/measure rename.

banned._noteHygiene: SS017 lexical hygiene (the anti-slop dict), SCOPED to names — not a string scraper. alwaysFlag = anthropomorphic/metaphor words refused in IDENTIFIERS (a Brain/Soul/Ouroboros TYPE is the ontology error: mechanism, never metaphor). NOT scraped from comments or string literals — prose may legitimately name a concept it tells you not to encode. commentPatterns = casual/inflammatory comment tells (lol/hacky/blazingly) refused in COMMENTS. contextWords are vague qualifiers (Legacy/New/V2/Temp); platform names (Win/Android/...) are legitimate and were removed 2026-06-17 (banning 'Android' in an Android project is absurd; mechanism-naming stays a convention, hosts are exempt). Both ride the census ratchet: existing hits baseline, NEW slop bleeds red. (litertlm-android/Com.Google.AI.Edge.Litertlm are now inert here — a banned DEPENDENCY belongs in a csproj/using check, not a word list; tracked with JNI removal.)
banned._note: Manager is NT-canonical and allowed. Banned applies to OUR public type names in core components; SDK attribute types (CmdletProviderAttribute etc.) are foreign surface, not ours. Hosts (heads) are exempt entirely - the host IS the context, and Android-mandated names (Services/*Service, *Activity) live there. 'Endpoint' refused per CONTRACT.md section 4. 'Store' refused: invariant 1 is 'no second store' - the sin's name cannot be a blessed suffix; use Hive (Cm-canonical) for durable planes, Table/Record/Block for data structures. Existing core violations (Pp's IProvisioningHandler, Hb's AgentSessionStore) ride the ratchet baseline.

platformNames._about: SS011 collision guard: declaring a type with one of these names is an error UNLESS it inherits the platform base class it names (class X : Android.App.Service) or is allow-listed by namespace (types.shipped covers Ss.Vom's deliberate NT analogs - our Port IS the ALPC analog, by design).

paths._about: SS004/SS014 vocabulary. Roots migrated from SS004FabricatedPathAnalyzer.cs:29 (ratchet R10: the analyzer reads THIS file, not its own list). Segments are the starter set pending the census.

drivers._about: Where the physical world enters - granted at construction, normally mounted at Root by a head.
